source.processed_dataset: Hooktheory.json.gz annotations.harmony is a separate conversion
source.representation: root (scale degree 1-7 in key) + type (Hookpad enum) + inversion + extensions

in_raw_chord_objects: root (diatonic scale degree, needs key to map to pitch class)
applied, adds, omits, alterations, suspensions, borrowed, pedal, alternate
not_in_raw_chord_objects: Per-chord MIDI pitches or octave placement
Open vs closed voicing / note spacing across octaves
Hookpad Band octave-centering or voicing style per chord
Explicit bass pitch separate from inversion (inversion implies bass tone)
related_elsewhere_in_raw_json: json.keys: tonic + scale for resolving scale degree → pitch class
json.notes: melody only (sd + octave), not chord voice leading
json.bands[].harmony/bass: instrument style names (playback preset), not note lists
processed_harmony_adds: Same inversion field preserved from raw
Still no register/voicing — only symbolic harmony
voicing_reconstruction.theorytab_playback: Hookpad voicing on the website is rendered by the playback engine from symbol + inversion + band settings; that rendered note list is not stored in Hooktheory_Raw.json or Hooktheory.json.gz.
